title: jovibarba hirta subsp. hirtella
intro: Jovibarba hirta subsp. hirtella, commonly known as the hairy winter aconite, is a rare, alpine plant species belonging to the Aconitum family. It possesses medicinal properties, primarily due to its high concentration of aconitine, a toxic yet potentially therapeutic alkaloid. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including pain, inflammation, and fever, as well as to promote wound healing and improve mental clarity. In herbal preparations, Jovibarba hirta subsp. hirtella is often used in homeopathic remedies and as a topical application for skin conditions, although its use requires extreme caution and expert guidance due to its toxicity.
